title: Smoking-related respiratory review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

source.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
source.population: People aged over 16 years with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
source.setting: Primary and specialist COPD diagnosis, monitoring, and ongoing management.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult COPD follow-up and exacerbation-feature documentation; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review

assessment.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
assessment.rationale: Supports tobacco exposure in adult chronic-cough history for smoking-related respiratory review; no decision is generated.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
source.population: Adults with chronic cough lasting more than 8 weeks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment of chronic cough.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic cough assessment and referral; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

assessment.text: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.
assessment.rationale: Supports smoking context in cough assessment for smoking-related respiratory review; no decision is generated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46
source.population: People presenting with cough; age limits are not stated in the applicability section.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth cough documentation; only partial for a general in-person workflow and for chronic-cough investigation pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.
